# Attest — Full Reference

> Delegated authorization protocol for AI agent pipelines.

Attest gives AI agents signed, scoped credentials tied to human intent. It lets you issue a root credential when a human approves a task, delegate narrower credentials to sub-agents, enforce tool-call authorization at runtime, and revoke the full delegation tree instantly. Every action produces a cryptographic evidence trail.

## Why Attest exists

AI agent frameworks (LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, OpenAI Agents SDK) ship tool-calling with no authorization layer. An agent with access to `gmail:send` can send any email to anyone. There is no way to prove what a human actually authorized, no way to scope what a sub-agent can do, and no way to revoke a running task tree.

Attest is the missing authorization protocol. It sits between the human approval and the tool call, ensuring every action is scoped, signed, and auditable.

## Core concepts

### Delegated authority
A human authorizes a task. The orchestrator receives a signed RS256 JWT credential scoped to that task. When it delegates work to a sub-agent, it issues a child credential with a strict subset of the parent's scope. Authority flows downward through the delegation tree, never sideways or upward.

### Intent binding
The human's original instruction is SHA-256 hashed and embedded in the credential as the `intent_hash` claim. Any mutation to the original intent is cryptographically detectable. This creates a tamper-evident link between what the human said and what the agent does.

### Scope enforcement
Each credential carries an explicit `scope` array (e.g. `["gmail:send", "research:read"]`). When a tool call is made, the enforcement layer checks that the requested action falls within the credential's scope. Child credentials must be strict subsets of their parent — you cannot escalate privileges through delegation.

### Revocation
Revoking a parent credential cascades to all children in the delegation tree. Revocation is checked at enforcement time via `GET /v1/revoked/{jti}`. A single API call can shut down an entire multi-agent task tree.

### Evidence and provenance
Every issuance, delegation, enforcement decision, tool call, and revocation is recorded in an append-only audit log. Each entry is hash-chained (each event references the previous event's hash), creating a tamper-evident evidence trail. Evidence can be exported as a signed packet and verified independently using the public verifier.

## API reference

Base URL: `https://api.attestdev.com`
Authentication: `Authorization: Bearer att_live_...`

### Organizations
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | /v1/orgs | Create an organization, returns API key |
| GET | /v1/org | Get current org details |

### Credentials
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | /v1/credentials | Issue a root credential |
| POST | /v1/credentials/delegate | Delegate a child credential |
| DELETE | /v1/credentials/{jti} | Revoke a credential and all children |
| GET | /v1/revoked/{jti} | Check revocation status |

### Audit and evidence
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | /v1/tasks/{tid}/audit | Query the audit trail for a task |

### Key management
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | /v1/org/keys | List signing keys |
| POST | /v1/org/keys | Import a signing key |
| DELETE | /v1/org/keys/{keyID} | Delete a signing key |
| POST | /v1/org/keys/rotate | Rotate to a new RSA-2048 key |

### JWKS
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | /orgs/{org_id}/jwks.json | Public JWKS endpoint for signature verification |

## TypeScript SDK

Install: `npm install @attest-dev/sdk@beta`
Package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@attest-dev/sdk

```typescript
import { AttestClient } from "@attest-dev/sdk";

const client = new AttestClient({
  baseUrl: "https://api.attestdev.com",
  apiKey: "att_live_...",
});

// Issue a root credential
const { token } = await client.issue({
  agent_id: "orchestrator-v1",
  user_id: "usr_alice",
  scope: ["research:read", "gmail:send"],
  instruction: "summarise inbox and send digest",
});

// Delegate to a sub-agent with narrower scope
const { token: childToken } = await client.delegate({
  parent_token: token,
  child_agent: "email-agent-v2",
  child_scope: ["gmail:send"],
});

// Revoke the entire delegation tree
await client.revoke(token);

// Verify a credential offline
const jwks = await client.fetchJWKS(orgId);
const result = await client.verify(token, jwks);
```

### SDK methods
- `client.issue(params)` — Issue a root credential. Params: `agent_id`, `user_id`, `scope`, `instruction`, optional `ttl_seconds`, `id_token`, `agent_checksum`.
- `client.delegate(params)` — Delegate a child credential. Params: `parent_token`, `child_agent`, `child_scope`.
- `client.revoke(token)` — Revoke a credential and all its children.
- `client.verify(token, jwks)` — Offline verification of signature, expiry, chain integrity.
- `client.fetchJWKS(orgId)` — Fetch org's public JWKS for verification.

## Python SDK

Install: `pip install attest-sdk`
Package: https://pypi.org/project/attest-sdk/

```python
from attest_sdk import AttestClient

client = AttestClient(
    base_url="https://api.attestdev.com",
    api_key="att_live_...",
)

token = client.issue(
    agent_id="orchestrator-v1",
    user_id="usr_alice",
    scope=["research:read", "gmail:send"],
    instruction="summarise inbox and send digest",
)
```

## MCP integration

Attest provides a drop-in MCP (Model Context Protocol) server wrapper that enforces credential-based authorization on every tool call. The wrapper intercepts `tools/call` requests, checks the credential's scope against the requested tool, and produces evidence for every allow/block decision.

Quickstart guide: https://attestdev.com/mcp/

## Credential format

Attest credentials are RS256-signed JWTs with these claims:
- `jti` — unique credential ID
- `sub` — agent ID
- `scope` — array of authorized actions
- `intent_hash` — SHA-256 of the human's instruction
- `depth` — delegation depth (0 for root)
- `chain` — array of ancestor credential IDs
- `exp` — expiry timestamp
- `tid` — task tree ID (shared across the delegation tree)

## IETF specification

Attest is specified as an IETF Internet-Draft: `draft-yakung-oauth-agent-attestation-00`. The spec defines the credential format, delegation protocol, scope narrowing rules, and revocation semantics as an extension to OAuth 2.0.

- API docs: https://attestdev.com/docs/
- MCP quickstart: https://attestdev.com/mcp/
- Evidence verifier: https://attestdev.com/verify/
- Interactive demo: https://attestdev.com/demo/
- GitHub: https://github.com/chudah1/attest-dev
- npm SDK: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@attest-dev/sdk
- PyPI SDK: https://pypi.org/project/attest-sdk/
- API health: https://api.attestdev.com/health
